summary: chai-defender fetches and executes remote code from server-genimi-check.vercel.app
details: chai-defender@1.0.0-beta.1 is a typosquat of chai-enzyme (impersonating the producthunt org and airbnb/enzyme references). src/assertions/checked.js contains a bootstrap() call that performs an HTTP GET to http://server-genimi-check.vercel.app/defy/v3, parses the JSON response, and passes the returned `token` string to `new (Function.constructor)('require', res.token)` before invoking it with the installer's `require`. This gives the remote endpoint arbitrary code execution in the Node process that loads the module. The payload is present only in src/ while the corresponding build/assertions/checked.js has been stripped to a benign assertion — a review-evasion tactic. Consumers who require the src path (e.g. via babel-register) or tools that resolve the src tree will execute the remote payload. The endpoint is served over plain HTTP, so both the maintainer and any on-path attacker can control the executed code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
